attributes.description: Alerts when the number of open file descriptors on a CouchDB node approaches the OS limit. Each open database file and network connection consumes a file descriptor; exhausting the limit causes new connections and database opens to fail.
attributes.name: [CouchDB OTel] File descriptor exhaustion risk

investigation_guide.blob:
## File Descriptor Exhaustion Risk

### Triage Steps
1. Check the open file descriptor trend on the CouchDB overview dashboard for the alerting node.
2. Cross-reference with `couchdb.database.open` — runaway database counts proportionally consume FDs.
3. Verify the OS-level `ulimit -n` setting on the CouchDB host: `cat /proc/{pid}/limits` for the `beam.smp` process.
4. Inspect active CouchDB connections — long-lived `_changes` feeds, replication tasks, and HTTP keep-alives all consume FDs.

### Remediation
- Raise the OS file descriptor limit (`ulimit -n`) and the systemd `LimitNOFILE` for the CouchDB service unit.
- Close idle replication tasks and unused `_changes` listeners.
- Identify and consolidate per-user or per-tenant databases if the open-database count is also high.
- Restart CouchDB during a maintenance window to release leaked descriptors if no application-side fix is possible.

### Threshold Tuning
The default threshold of 1000 open FDs assumes a typical `ulimit -n` of 1024–65536. Set the threshold to ~70-80% of your configured limit to give time to react before exhaustion.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-couchdbreceiver.otel-default
// Open file descriptors is a gauge — use LAST_OVER_TIME for current state
| WHERE couchdb.file_descriptor.open IS NOT NULL
| STATS open_fds = MAX(LAST_OVER_TIME(couchdb.file_descriptor.open))
  BY couchdb.node.name
// Typical ulimit is 1024–65536 — adjust threshold for your environment
| WHERE open_fds > 1000
| SORT open_fds DESC
| LIMIT 20
